I want to say probably towards the end of last year and What audience insight does is you type in an interest into Facebook? And it'll tell you all of the people who like that interest and then what pages they like It'll tell you the demographics how old these individuals are it'll tell you if they're male or female It'll tell you if they're married or single So I'm going to go and show you guys what audience insights look like on looks like on Facebook And then also how to use audience insights All right, so can everyone see this All right, so in Facebook if you go inside the ads manager, there'll be a tab called audience insights and Once you open this up It'll give you an option to choose everyone on Facebook. You can choose a custom audience So to create a custom audience, let's say if you guys have an email list You have an email list of five thousand or ten thousand people you can upload your email list into Facebook and they'll tell you what the you know what pages they like what the age demographics of Of your email list is so you can get a lot of information on there But let me go ahead and show you guys how you can just find out information if you don't know it just yet about your particular audience so I'm gonna go over here and click everyone and Right now I Wish I could make this bigger for you guys, but I'm gonna go ahead and make it Make it work. Okay, not a lot of hands so what what Ashley Madison is about is You don't have to admit it so I'm gonna say listen for those of you who don't know what Ashley Madison is a site for I Don't condone it, but it's for people who you know are married or they're in a relationship and they would like to have an affair Okay There's people out there like that, but um you can actually find this audience on Facebook So I'm gonna type in Ashley Madison It's an interest on Facebook and it has about 300,000 people that like Ashley Madison, okay? I Can see the ages of the ages of the people I can see the demographics of its male or female They have lifestyle categories and then also Relationship status so as you can see 45% of these people are married and they have an interest in the page Ashley Madison So let's say if I want to target people who are interested in Having an affair and if I want to run let's say a CPA ad to individuals You know for this Ashley Madison website I can find men between the ages of 18 and 65 and over who are interested in this website Ashley Madison All right, but it doesn't stop there because what else I can do is I can go after men if I scroll down here to the advanced category I can go after men whose relationship statuses are either engaged or married and It'll show me the audience size up here, which is around 45 to 50,000 people So if I want to go after men and run a specific ad geared towards these individuals I can do that and the way that you would do that is You would come up here Because Facebook already has the data already over here I Would come up here and I would save this audience so I'll click save as and I'll save this audience as let's say Ashley Madison And what that'll allow me to do is go into ads manager and create ads With this particular audience I can split this ad up into people between the ages of 18 and 24 Between the ages of 25 and 35 and I can just run those ads to those individuals All right, so enough of that. All right, and you just what I do with this is I take about 30 minutes 45 minutes just playing around in here growing my audience so that I can get a very niche audience to run my ads to all right, so That's just a little bit of information about the audience insights and how you go about creating your audience Later, I'll show you how this plays into my secret tool because what I'm going to do is usually when I create an ad I'll have a ton of different Interests and I don't run my ads to want to all of those interests at one time I break them out because I want to see what interests are going to convert and we'll talk about that in the case study as well but let me go ahead and hop back over here into the presentation All right, so Now that we have our audience we know who our potential customers are Now the next step is going to be we need to create an ad now in Facebook you have three different types of ads and Here's what we're going to talk about we're going to talk about targeting those users with those specific type of ads All right, how many you got who knows what type of ad this is? It's a newsfeed add any specific type of newsfeed ad Okay, it's a it's a promoted post But what I like to call these are link ads and the reason why I like to call these link ads is because when someone clicks on this It takes them directly to the webpage Okay, these are great for webinars Because when someone sees it in a newsfeed they're going to see that image and they're going to click on it And as soon as they click on it it takes them to the registration page So I love using these for different types of products webinars Anything you want to sell the newsfeed ads are great and because they go directly to the sales page Of course, you're going to want to make sure that in your newsfeed ad or your link ad you're going to have your copy above the ad Usually telling individuals to click the link below in order to send them directly to that page Then also right here, this is a sponsored ad this is what you call I like to call this a page post ad Some people like to call it a photo page post ad because like the other one that I showed you before When they click on this image, it doesn't take them to the website It takes them directly to a photo So when they click on that it'll take them to a photo the photo are enlarged and then if they click right It'll scroll to other photos that's on your page I don't like to run me use these for web webinars because as I mentioned if they click the photo It's going to take them to other images. That's not what I want I want to take them directly to my registration page so that I can get them on that webinar So I don't like to use these for webinars if you ever do use these One thing that they're good for is like brand recognition like I would use this for retargeting at if I had a product I would use this type of image so I can stay in front of those individuals with the retarget ad Also, if I do use something along these lines, I want to make sure that I put my link in the post So where your copy is up top you want to make sure you have a link there so that people know where to go If there is a product that you're promoting with that photo, let them know that the link is below, you know or above or whatnot And then right here a lot of people how many you guys are using right-side ads a Lot of people Kind of shied away from right-side ads because in the past they they didn't do too well but just recently Facebook enlarged the sizes of the right-side ad and These ads are amazing. They work great for people if you're targeting people who are in internet marketing right-side ads are they're great because these individuals are usually on their desktops and Right-side ads only show up on desktops Also, it's good I don't know why because I thought that older people use like iPads all the time But for some reason I get a lot of 45 enough males clicking on right-side ads so I mean a lot of a lot of the women they click on They're there they're on mobile so like older women. They're usually on mobile but older males You can use right-side ads to target them. So here's a couple of samples of Right-side ads that popped up on my news feed Got one from Dollar Shave Club like they advertise like crazy Their retargeting is pretty good a couple of other ones up there But right-side ads they're gonna click funnels that actually Right-side ads are they work very well. I love them So if you want to target your customer you have three options Well, actually you have four because you have video as well But you have your link ads which are good for webinars You have your page post ads which are good for like products and stand in front of your customers And then your right-side ads now one thing I like to do is you have your link ads and right-side ads When you create them they can actually be the same ad inside of Facebook because they're actually the same size But I like to make sure that when I create my right-side ad that my my headline is shorter Because if it's a longer headline then it's not going to show up in that right side and it's going to be cut off But if it's a shorter headline like let's say crack new traffic methods They can see that and then also if you look at the description Below they can see all of that in the right-side ad as well But if you use your link ad and it has longer text, it's going to be cut off All right, so Once you decide on what type of ad you like to run Here's an actual ad that um that I ran and one of the campaigns that I've done it was selling a clickbank product on Facebook and the parts of the ad that I like to let people know about is The first sentence of your ad I like to have it be a question because I like to spark people's interests so in this one it says would you like to turn your photos into amazing works of art and the product that I was promoting here was a Photoshop tutorials, which was a clickbank product and Below that I like to tell them what they will learn in the course So I like to ask the question Tell them what to expect and then I give them bullets and below there it'll have one two three four and It'll just tell them like what they can expect in the ad Below that it'll be a call to action letting them know hey if you click here This is what'll happen Then you have images Your images you want to make sure that these images relate to your niche So let's say if this one right here. It's going towards photographers So I have a lady with a camera this worked extremely well I was getting clicks like two to three two to three two cents to 13 cents You know with certain niches if you use that image that relates to them They're really going to respond to it. So what it usually if you're running a webinar I recommend either niche specific images or if you want to go with branded Then you can you know post images of yourself and with Liz images of herself really worked well and Then lastly Headlines I Really couldn't tell you guys like what's going to work for its headlines because it's different from niche to niche and The only way that you're going to find out what headline works is going to be to create a couple of them and then test it And I know that may seem like it's going to be a long task But this tool that I'm going to show you guys in the case and in a bit It's going to show you how you can test all your headlines pretty easy Everybody following along cool and Then below your headline Which it says advanced Photoshop tutorials for photographers it says you don't need a $3,000 camera or a 1500 lens You can basically just add on to you know your your copy at the top below there So How do you go about finding a copy for your specific niche? I'm not a copywriter Actually when Liz actually asked me to run traffic for her webinar never ran webinar traffic before So what I did was I went out there and I found ads in that specific niche and I copied them I basically you know got the idea I Swipe created a swipe file I went through the news feed and I swiped a couple of images of different web webinars or products that people were promoting and I basically saw what was working for them and I kind of like You know geared up our ads that was similar to theirs I created about three different variations of each and then we basically put it in the program and it mixed it up for us And we were able to see what exactly what worked so here's a couple of images These are the exact ones that I mimicked in the webinar ads that we ran for her I just went through a couple of news feed ads I screen shot them and then we just went ahead and we created them So yeah, what's your question? All right, so another thing if you want to create find news feed ads for your niche You this is what I would recommend and do go to the audience insights See what pages people in your niche like and then go like them yourself All right, and also I mean out if you don't if you don't want to like you know a hundred pages Just click on them because what Facebook does is if you actually click on a page I'll say that's dealing with guns You're gonna start seeing gun pages in your news feed or yeah gun gun ads or gun sponsorships in your news feed And the reason for that is because that's how Facebook does their algorithm far as how you can target individuals through their ad platform so if you click and I don't know if this has a A laser on it, but if you if you look in your news feed You can actually see how they're targeting you So in the top right hand corner of one of these ads you'll click an X and you'll you'll click Why am I seeing this ad and you'll see how this person is targeting you sometimes it can be because the people who are in a specific Area it'll be because you have it It'll say you have an interest in guns if you clicked on a gun page So this is how you'll you'll be able to start seeing those ads in your news feed. How many of you guys have ever heard of Koya? That's great not too many hands It's called Koya. I Don't know if I'm saying it right. I just know that that's what it looks like It's QW AYA and I'm about to show you guys how to use it actually in a minute. So Koya is a Actually, let me log in to Koya so I can actually show you guys better than I can tell you because it actually lets me Create these ads so that I can go ahead and I can run 300 or so ads at a time so as I mentioned it's QW a Y a and I'm actually going to show you how I run ads on Facebook and I create different variations of these ads Gotcha Koya is a it's a third-party program and what it does is it connects to the Facebook API and it allows you to create ads and Upload them to your ad account. So I never I never even log into Facebook anymore I don't log into Facebook to check my ads. Everything gets done inside of Koya So what I'm about to do right now is I'm going to create some ads so First all you have to do in Koya is you come to your account and You type whatever page you want to run a specific add-on. So I'm going to run an ad on my my page Which is Mike Nelson and I'm going to create a link ad And we talked about link ads. It's where when you click that ad it'll go directly to a website So this link ad that I'm about to create. then it's going to say add post link This is where you want to direct your customer So if it's a webinar it's going to be to the webinar registration page You're going to click add this link right once I create this one time with all of those different links Let's say if it's five different links in there it'll create five different ads to that specific link So if I have five images, it'll create five Five different ads with five different images with five different links Okay, so you guys can see where I'm going with this like if I want to if I put five images It's going to create five different ads if I put five different images with five different links It's going to create 25 different ads So this is how you're able to you know create multiple ads so you can do the split testing for your for your webinar so Post-tex as I showed you in the demonstration I like to start my ads off with a question Then I give them what they can expect on the webinar and then we go into bullet like It's going to be right there underneath the picture Usually what I like to tell people in here is like hey free webinar and then the webinar is named So I'm going to copy this And then the description Usually what we like to tell them in the description is what time the ad or what time the webinar is going to take place Or you can give them more details from the copy up above and you can put that in the description of the ad And don't worry you guys will be able to see exactly what this looks like as soon as soon as I'm done here And then I have five different ad images So this is how you're going to test to see what ad images are converting for on your website You want it when you always running that you're not going to want to just run one image I don't care your copy can be the same your link headline can be the same But the images in my opinion the images are what gets people to click on your ads and when I do the case study You'll see exactly like what type of ads work best for us In order and which ones convert because of the ad images So I'm going to select five different ad images that I created And what you can do is you know if you create the images is very simple All you got to do is just choose the files and then open them up inside of Coya. It'll upload them in there for you and The last step is going to be selecting if you want it to be a desktop ad Either a desktop newsfeed or desktop right-side ad if you want it to be a mobile newsfeed or mobile Or a mobile newsfeed an audience network ad or just mobile newsfeed only when I start off my testing Like if it's a new audience that we're running traffic to when I start that off It's going to just be to the mobile desktop and newsfeed and they're going to all be in the same ad set So we're going to do this desktop and mobile newsfeed and Then we're going to create the ad and once I create it What you're going to see is that now I have five different ads with all the same copy the same links same headlines but different ad images and You can get really creative in here You can run it where if I just want to run ads to desktops with the same images and then I can run ads to New mobile with the same images so you can split it up as well inside of Coya It actually it gets pretty cool like as you start to have more images and different copy in there as well so from there What I'm going to do is I'm going to create my targeting So I have the images ready now. They're on my page now I need to find the audience to target so let's say if I was running to add a Webinar to a business audience the first thing that you're going to want to do is Come over here and do your locations So if I want to target people that are in the United States, I can either select United States in Canada I can split those ads up which you know and I'll show you in the case study You'll realize that people in different countries they buy so you can target your whole You can target Canada you can target United States you can target United Kingdom in different ads So you don't have to have them all mixed up. So that's pretty cool as well. So I'm just typing United States and Then I'm going to type in Canada as well So let's go ahead and do Canada and then I can choose to either run them all in the same ad set or I can choose to split them I'm going to go ahead and I'm going to choose to split them and what you're going to see down here is that it's going to split Those audiences if you can see right here in the bottom right it's going to split them into two different segments And what that means is when it splits it into two different segments That means that I'm going to have those five different ads those ads are going to go into two different to two different countries So now I'm going to have a total of ten ads if I was to add another country I'll have 15 ads so that that's how you really find out Who's buying from what particular ads because you're going to start adding those conversion pixels as well and Then you can select languages. I always just leave that blank. It's automatically at English and Here's a good part. So let's say if you know that your audience is between the ages of 25 and 45 You can hear what you can do is you can split that up Actually, it'll be 25 to 44 you can split that up by By years so if I wanted to split this by male and female or if I wanted to split it up by and by every Five years I can do it to people who are between the ages of 25 and 20 and 25 and 30 I can do it from 31 to 34 So now what I'm doing is is I'm going to find out, you know, even more deeper information about these people because In the case study you'll see Certain age groups Purchase more than others and that was one thing I never understood when I got in when I you know before I knew about marketing Was how can you market to you know people who are 18 and 24 and you know that they're going to buy? Well, you're going to know that they're going to buy because you can track all of that information inside of Facebook So I'm just going to run this to males and females. I'm not going to split it and now I'm going to run it to different interests. So You Yeah, you can actually it's supposed to be a Drop-down menu. I'm not sure why it's not showing up there, but it'll give you you can choose every year you want So let's see if we if we want to do interest and let's go back to our one that we used earlier What what it'll also do is It'll give you other websites That these people would possibly be interested in and now you can start adding those websites into your your audience that you're going to create So So people try it, you know CPC. We've tried that doesn't work as well Optimized CPM doesn't work as well But when I do cost per per conversion it works very well with any ad that I run is and or if you're not getting any traffic bid higher So that's all I do if any track no traffic is coming through raise it up Some people like to raise it up a couple cents I'll raise it up about ten twenty dollars to get that ad to start getting impressions and then once I see that it's you know It's working if it is working. I'll keep it out bump it up some more if it's not I'll bump it down All right, so right here when I'm gonna start off at is $30. So I'm telling Facebook I'm willing to spend $30 per conversion that they send me. I never usually I never pay anywhere close to $30 I usually get conversions at like between the dollar and $4 so they never spend that if your ad is pretty good if it sucks Facebook is going to charge you about $30 But I never had that problem Yeah, so I'm not gonna be able to show you that right now, but your pixel is going to be inside your ads manager Inside Facebook it gives you the ability to create that conversion pixel and you can create it for your website In click funnels We just place it on the webinar checkout page and then also what you want to make sure you do is you put that pixel on Thank you page But then you also put it on if you're gonna have a webinar put it on your webinar Purchase thank you page so now you can see what interest they come from you can see how old they are You can see you know if that if that converted into an actual sale from what particular interest as well Yeah, you can get the tracking codes from Coya you can create it in here Or if you want to you can create it in Facebook as well in order to use that tracking code all you do is you come over here and You create that tracking code it has Google analytics and you can create the Variable links to track all your ads as well when we sell our t-shirts We use variable links to see exactly what age group these individuals are coming from if they're coming from like 25 to 34 or 35 to 44 that helps us as well because it gives us Facebook stats, but then we're also tracking our own stats as well to see exactly where the sales are coming from and Then once you put all your tracking in place, I didn't put a budget So I'll put a budget of $20 per day for each ad set and as you can see I have four different ad sets And it's going to charge me you know $80 a day because it's four different ones and the reason why it's four different ad sets It's four ad sets, but each ad set has five images in it So they're five different ads and what we're telling Facebook is hey optimize all of these ads to get us conversions and a little tip is actually I found out that It doesn't matter what ad it what ad is pulling all of the conversions for you because if you try to run it over and over again Facebook will stop sending it traffic. That's what that's what we realize But every time we run a new webinar I like to put at least eight ad images Inside one ad set to the interest because what happens is once one ad starts slowing down Facebook has seven more that that's going to run traffic to so seven more that may not have stats And that's what actually keeps our conversion rate down unless we bump our budget up So if we just keep it at a certain amount and it has eight different ad images in that ad set Facebook will optimize it and we can get conversions for webinars around a dollar a dollar fifty to two dollars all day So that's actually that's that's a quick tip for you guys Now what happened is all of those? All of my ads are created right now I can run all of those ad sets to those different interests or those different Audiences and all I have to do now is just publish to Facebook and now it'll be uploaded to my account So if I wanted this to go live and I wanted to put 20 ads into my account it'll go ahead and it'll start doing that automatically Yeah, everything is done through Koya Far as placing the conversion pixels creating your audiences creating the actual ads uploading the images I don't log into Koya in the Facebook anymore. I can actually track all of my and it's better I can track all of my ads inside of Koya and the good thing is inside of Koya. I can schedule ads you can day part So if I want to run ads only at 10 a.m. To 11 a 11 p.m. I can do that and one way that I can run ads and not have to worry about them is you can put rules on them So I can create 300 ads and I can put a rule to where if the conversion cost is above $20 then stop that ad and Facebook will stop the ad and then it'll just run all of the other ones So it's no way we can lose money, you know once you start knowing your numbers by using Koya I honestly I don't look at cost per click. I don't look at click-through rate all I look at is conversions and costs And then after that like when I'm starting to add that's all I look at but then after that What we start to do is you know exactly how much that interest has made you so now you can start to spend more Per conversion on that ad When you're running webinar traffic, I mean honestly cost per click like some of our click-through rates are at like 2% to me You know in other businesses, we're able to see click-through rates at 7 But if I'm rating it on that then we wouldn't be making any money because I'm gonna try to get a 7% click-through rate And I can't but I can get you know a 2% click-through rate, but 159 opt-ins at $3 So it just mad it just it all matters on you know opt-ins and then if they're converting into actual sales That's what I would worry about. Trust me so The case study I did was is that's actually me and Liz right there I actually met Liz on Facebook. She shot me a message and we ended up talking on Skype and We decided to become accountability partners and as I was talking to her we was setting our goals She let me know that she had a webinar, but she couldn't get traffic to be ran to the webinar and I said hey Liz, you know what I Can run traffic I've never ran webinar traffic But I want to help you because I know that you have the ability to be successful because you know your personality You know your stuff, you know, I want to help you out. So after we got off that call we decided to work together and After I got off that call actually didn't know what I got myself into because I didn't run webinar traffic ever before so I didn't know if we're gonna be successful or if we're gonna fail But from the stats that I'm gonna show you guys right now We had a little bit of success and I want to show you guys that So here's our stats This is a webinar that we ran on December 12th or December 4th of 2014 we had a total of 1439 web registrations these are people who clicked on our ad and they came over and a register for the webinar the people who actually Showed up to that webinar was 333 or 330 people actually showed up and From that webinar we had a total of 50 cells now You guys when you guys look at the profit take take note of one thing and when you look at the ad spend take note of one thing of these total cells It's actually going to be more in profit because some of these cells actually They're there on the plan Correct they're on a plan so it's gonna actually be more than cells in that but the revenue generated was $30,000 not including all the individuals that you know gonna pay in the future and our advertisement costs It was a fifty seven hundred dollars All right roughly the good thing I like about running ads for Liz is that Her currency is in New Zealand and we're making money in the US So I'm looking at these converters and it's like a dollar seventy well actually if you convert it over in the US It's like way less than that. It's just like it's amazing But our total profit for this webinar was $25,000 Right, but this isn't the impressive part. I mean how many of you guys would like to spend $5,000 and make 25,000 All right, I'm pretty sure you guys would do this every day, but that's good But it's not great. We didn't even target just that interest So once we start running these ads again We can run one ad to that specific interest to that specific age group and now you can do even more things You can spend less money and convert more so now you're going to start making more now your business is going to start making more And that's when it starts to get good so we spent $427 we had 12 conversions And that came from 109 registrations but of those Of those 12 registrations ten of them came from the age group of 35 and 44 year olds Okay, and of those people who register that's in this category ten of them But so that's 25% of people who register for the webinar from that particular ad ten of those people actually purchased the product All right, so What you can see and you can't you guys can't see this I'm a break it down to you is that six of those cells in That specific interest came from people who purchased on an iPad So what this tells me is that now if I want to target people in Australia and Canada in that specific age group I can just run mobile ads Mobile ads directly to those people because they're going to buy from their their mobile or I can just run ads to Specifically people who are running on iPads and you can do that inside of Facebook You can run it to specific people who are running on mobile or desktop or iPads or using a specific Apple device or using a droid phone You can set that up in Koya or you can set it up inside of the ads manager that it doesn't matter but you can set it up in Alright, so you guys you guys impressed is that those some good numbers?
